pairs.question: What was the prize for winning the IAAF Golden League jackpot in 2000?
pairs.answer: The prize for winning the IAAF Golden League jackpot in 2000 was 50 kilograms of gold bars.

pairs.question: How many meetings were part of the 2000 IAAF Golden League series?
pairs.answer: The 2000 IAAF Golden League series consisted of seven European meetings: Meeting Gaz de France, Golden Gala, Bislett Games, Weltklasse Zürich, Herculis, Memorial Van Damme, and Internationales Stadionfest (ISTAF).

pairs.question: Which athletes won the Golden League jackpot in 2000, and in which events did they compete?
pairs.answer: The Golden League jackpot winners in 2000 were Maurice Greene (100 m), Hicham El Guerrouj (1500 m), Trine Hattestad (javelin throw), and Tatyana Kotova (long jump).

pairs.question: What were the jackpot events for men in the 2000 IAAF Golden League?
pairs.answer: The jackpot events for men in the 2000 IAAF Golden League were: 100 m, 1500 m, 3000 m, 400 m hurdles, high jump, pole vault, and shot put.

pairs.question: Which athlete set two world records during the 2000 Golden League series, and in which events?
pairs.answer: Trine Hattestad of Norway set two world records during the 2000 Golden League series, with throws of 68.22 meters in the javelin at the Golden Gala and 69.48 meters at the Bislett Games.

pairs.question: What doping-related incident occurred at the Bislett Games in the 2000 Golden League?
pairs.answer: Shot putter CJ Hunter tested positive for doping at the Bislett Games, and all his results from that meeting onwards were annulled.

pairs.question: How were the jackpot winners determined in the 2000 IAAF Golden League?
pairs.answer: Athletes could win the jackpot if they won at least five of the seven competitions in one of the 12 specified events (7 for men, 5 for women). Only those who met this criterion were eligible for the prize.
